corpus_notes: CRITICAL EDGE CASE: This is a food nutrition panel, NOT a supplement facts panel. Uses AU FSANZ format with per-serving AND per-100g columns. Completely different format from TGA supplement labels. Tests: (1) Recognition of food nutrition panel format, (2) Energy (kJ) row — not a nutrient entry, (3) Calories row — not a nutrient entry, (4) Per-100g column — ignore for analysis (use per-serving), (5) %RDI column present on label itself — parser must extract raw amounts, not trust label's own RDI%. (6) CGF (Chlorella Growth Factor) — no NRV, not a standard nutrient. (7) DNA/RNA content — no NRV. Serving-size-dependent analysis — unusual for supplement.

expected_entries_per_serving.name: Protein
expected_entries_per_serving.note: Macronutrient — no NRV for protein in supplement context

expected_entries_per_serving.name: Vit D
expected_entries_per_serving.note: Label shows 34mcg at 204% RDI — but per-serving is 1.13mcg. Reconcile: 34mcg appears to be per-100g value accidentally placed in per-serving column. Use 1.13mcg per serving.

per_100g_column_note: Ignore per-100g column for analysis. Use per-serving column only.

clinical_notes.food_source_note: Chlorella is a food supplement — vitamin content is naturally occurring, not added. Bioavailability may differ from synthetic forms.
clinical_notes.serving_size_note: 6 tablets = 1 serve. Users often take fewer tablets. Report should note per-tablet values when serving adjustment is made.
clinical_notes.drug_interactions_flag: Vitamin K content (even at 1.11mcg/serve) — advise patients on warfarin to maintain consistent chlorella intake as K affects INR.
